general_skills.title: Verify Before Mutating
general_skills.principle: Always read the current state of an Odoo record before creating, updating, or deleting it. Confirm the operation with the user when it affects production data.
general_skills.application: Before any write/create/delete call, do a search_read to confirm the target exists and is in the expected state. Ask for explicit user confirmation on destructive operations.

general_skills.title: Use Domain Filters Precisely
general_skills.principle: Construct Odoo domains with the most specific filters possible to avoid retrieving excessive data or acting on wrong records.
general_skills.application: Always include state, type, and date filters when querying models like account.move, stock.picking, or sale.order. Prefer exact matches over broad searches.

general_skills.title: Check Model Availability
general_skills.principle: Not all Odoo models are available on every instance — modules may not be installed. Verify model existence before querying.
general_skills.application: Use odoo_list_models or odoo_get_fields to confirm the model exists. If a model is missing, explain which module needs to be installed.

general_skills.title: Batch Operations Safely
general_skills.principle: When processing multiple records, work in small batches and report progress. Never process unbounded result sets in a single call.
general_skills.application: Limit search_read results with a reasonable limit (50-100). For write operations on many records, process in chunks of 10-20 and confirm intermediate results.

general_skills.title: Interpret Odoo Errors Contextually
general_skills.principle: Odoo errors often contain technical tracebacks. Translate them into actionable explanations for the user, identifying the root cause and suggesting fixes.
general_skills.application: When an RPC call fails, parse the error message for common patterns (access rights, missing fields, constraint violations) and explain in plain language.

general_skills.title: Respect the Odoo Workflow
general_skills.principle: Odoo records follow state machines (draft -> confirmed -> done). Never skip states by directly writing state fields — use the appropriate action methods.
general_skills.application: Use action_post for invoices, action_confirm for sale orders, button_validate for stock pickings. Never write {'state': 'posted'} directly.

general_skills.title: Progressive Field Discovery
general_skills.principle: When working with unfamiliar models or investigating data quality, use odoo_get_fields first to understand available fields before querying records.
general_skills.application: Start with odoo_get_fields to see field types and relationships, then construct targeted search_read queries with only the fields needed for analysis.

general_skills.title: Multi-Angle Investigation
general_skills.principle: For thorough analysis, examine the same issue from multiple perspectives using related models and cross-references.
general_skills.application: When checking module states, also query ir.module.module.dependency for dependency issues. When auditing cron jobs, check both ir.cron and ir.logging for execution history.

general_skills.title: Combine Diagnostic Tools Strategically
general_skills.principle: Use odoo_doctor as the initial broad sweep, then follow up with targeted model queries to drill into specific findings. This two-phase approach is more efficient than single-tool queries.
general_skills.application: Start with odoo_doctor to get a system overview. Based on its output, use odoo_search_read on specific models (ir.cron, ir.module.module, ir.logging) to investigate flagged areas in detail.

general_skills.title: Aggregate and Summarize for Clarity
general_skills.principle: When presenting findings from multiple queries, organize results into categorized tables or summaries (OK / Warning / Critical) to help users quickly identify priorities.
general_skills.application: Group related findings by severity level. Present counts, totals, and key metrics in tabular format. Highlight critical items first, then warnings, then informational items.

general_skills.title: Temporal Filtering for Audit Trails
general_skills.principle: When investigating recent events or changes, use date/time filters to narrow scope and improve query performance. Always specify time windows for audit queries.
general_skills.application: Use create_date, write_date, or custom date fields with comparison operators (>, <, >=, <=). For 24-hour lookbacks, calculate datetime.now() - timedelta(hours=24). Include timezone awareness in date comparisons.

general_skills.title: Leverage search_count for Efficiency
general_skills.principle: When you only need to know the quantity of records matching a condition, use search_count instead of search_read to reduce data transfer and improve performance.
general_skills.application: Use odoo_search_count to get record counts before deciding whether to fetch full details. Useful for reporting metrics, detecting anomalies (e.g., 'are there any negative stock records?'), and validating data quality thresholds.

general_skills.title: Combine Count and Detail Queries Strategically
general_skills.principle: Use search_count first to assess scope, then conditionally fetch details only if counts warrant investigation. This reduces unnecessary data transfer.
general_skills.application: Query search_count with a filter to get magnitude of issue. If count is high, fetch details with limit and offset. If count is zero, report 'no issues found' immediately without additional queries.

general_skills.title: Cross-Reference Related Models for Completeness
general_skills.principle: When investigating a specific model (e.g., ir.cron), always check related audit/logging models (ir.logging) to get the full picture of recent activity and failures.
general_skills.application: For cron jobs, query both ir.cron (state) and ir.logging (execution history). For modules, query both ir.module.module (state) and ir.module.module.dependency (dependency health). This reveals hidden issues.

general_skills.title: Build Dependency Graphs Iteratively
general_skills.principle: When exploring dependency relationships (e.g., module dependencies), query parent-child relationships iteratively rather than trying to fetch the entire tree at once.
general_skills.application: Start with a target module, fetch its direct dependencies via ir.module.module.dependency. For each dependency, recursively fetch its dependencies. Use a visited set to avoid cycles. Present results as a hierarchical structure or adjacency list.

general_skills.title: Validate Data Consistency Across Related Models
general_skills.principle: When investigating module or system state, cross-validate findings across multiple related models to detect inconsistencies that indicate deeper issues.
general_skills.application: For module checks: verify state in ir.module.module matches expected installation status, check ir.module.module.dependency for broken links, and query ir.model for orphaned model definitions. Report any mismatches as warnings.

general_skills.title: Optimize Query Sequences with Early Termination
general_skills.principle: Structure diagnostic queries to fail fast: use search_count to detect zero-result conditions before fetching full records, avoiding unnecessary data transfer.
general_skills.application: When investigating a category of issues (e.g., error logs), first search_count to determine if any records exist. If count is 0, report immediately. If count is high, fetch with limit. This pattern reduces wasted queries.

general_skills.title: Understand Field Metadata for Complete Analysis
general_skills.principle: When analyzing model fields, extract and interpret metadata (required, readonly, selection values, field types) to provide comprehensive guidance on field behavior and constraints.
general_skills.application: Use odoo_get_fields to retrieve field definitions including 'required', 'readonly', 'selection', 'relation', and 'type'. Present this metadata clearly to users so they understand field constraints and dependencies.

general_skills.title: Distinguish Between Runtime and Code-Level Customizations
general_skills.principle: When advising on model customizations, clearly differentiate between changes that can be made at runtime (via UI or API) versus those requiring code/module development.
general_skills.application: For attributes like _order, _rec_name, or computed fields: explain that these are defined in Python code and cannot be changed at runtime. For window actions, contexts, and field properties: use odoo_modify_action to make runtime changes. Always clarify the scope of what's possible.

general_skills.title: Enumerate Selection Field Values Comprehensively
general_skills.principle: When asked about selection fields, extract all possible values from field definitions and cross-reference with actual data to ensure completeness and identify unused options.
general_skills.application: Use odoo_get_fields to retrieve the 'selection' attribute for selection fields. Then query the model with search_read to find all distinct values actually present in records. Report both defined and observed values, highlighting any discrepancies.

task_specific_skills.title: Analyze Model Structure Comprehensively
task_specific_skills.principle: When analyzing a model, gather complete structural information including field definitions, relationships, metadata, and system attributes in a single coherent investigation.
task_specific_skills.application: Use odoo_model_info to get model metadata, odoo_get_fields to extract field definitions with types and constraints, and odoo_search_read to examine actual data. Synthesize findings into a clear picture of model structure and behavior.

task_specific_skills.title: Identify and Filter Related Fields by Type
task_specific_skills.principle: When searching for fields of a specific type (Many2one, One2many, selection, etc.), use field metadata to filter precisely rather than scanning all fields manually.
task_specific_skills.application: Use odoo_get_fields and filter results by 'type' attribute. For Many2one fields, also check the 'relation' attribute to identify target models. Present results in a structured format showing field name, type, and relation.

task_specific_skills.title: Modify Window Actions with Precision
task_specific_skills.principle: When customizing window actions (context, domain, groupby), use odoo_modify_action to make targeted changes without affecting other action properties.
task_specific_skills.application: Use odoo_modify_action to add or update context values, default domains, or groupby settings. Always verify the action exists first with odoo_model_info. Confirm changes with the user before applying.

task_specific_skills.title: Explain Model Attribute Scope and Mutability
task_specific_skills.principle: When users ask about model attributes, clearly explain whether they are code-level (immutable at runtime) or configuration-level (modifiable via UI/API).
task_specific_skills.application: For _order, _rec_name, _description: explain these are Python class attributes defined in module code and require code changes. For window actions, contexts, domains: explain these are database records modifiable via odoo_modify_action. For field properties: explain which are code-level vs. configurable.

common_mistakes.mistake: Assuming all Odoo models are available on every instance
common_mistakes.consequence: Queries fail with 'model not found' errors, wasting turns and confusing users.
common_mistakes.prevention: Always call odoo_list_models or odoo_get_fields first to verify model existence before querying. If a model is missing, identify which module provides it.

common_mistakes.mistake: Writing state fields directly instead of using workflow action methods
common_mistakes.consequence: Violates Odoo's state machine constraints, causing validation errors or data corruption.
common_mistakes.prevention: Always use the appropriate action method (action_post, action_confirm, button_validate) instead of directly writing state. Verify the correct method exists via odoo_get_fields.

common_mistakes.mistake: Fetching all records without limits when only a count is needed
common_mistakes.consequence: Wastes bandwidth and API calls; slows down responses unnecessarily.
common_mistakes.prevention: Use odoo_search_count when you only need to know if records exist or how many match a condition. Reserve odoo_search_read for when you need actual data.

common_mistakes.mistake: Constructing overly broad domain filters that match unintended records
common_mistakes.consequence: Operates on wrong records, causing data corruption or incorrect analysis.
common_mistakes.prevention: Always include specific filters (state, type, date ranges) in domains. Test domains with search_count first to verify they match the intended scope.

common_mistakes.mistake: Attempting to modify code-level model attributes (like _order) at runtime
common_mistakes.consequence: User confusion; wasted effort trying to change immutable attributes.
common_mistakes.prevention: Distinguish between code-level attributes (require module development) and runtime-configurable properties (use odoo_modify_action). Explain this distinction clearly to users.

common_mistakes.mistake: Assuming selection field values without checking field definitions
common_mistakes.consequence: Incomplete or inaccurate reporting of available options.
common_mistakes.prevention: Always extract selection values from odoo_get_fields output. Cross-reference with actual data via search_read to identify all observed values, including any not in the field definition.

common_mistakes.mistake: Processing large result sets in a single query without batching
common_mistakes.consequence: Timeout errors, memory issues, or incomplete processing.
common_mistakes.prevention: Always use limit and offset in search_read. For write operations on many records, process in chunks of 10-20 and report progress.

common_mistakes.mistake: Ignoring related models when investigating a specific model's state
common_mistakes.consequence: Incomplete diagnosis; missing root causes or hidden issues.
common_mistakes.prevention: Use multi-angle investigation (GS-008): for cron jobs check ir.logging, for modules check dependencies, for moves check related documents. Cross-validate findings across related models.
